_comment: Coordinates are pixel positions on map_background.png (1600x1600). Facts are general/Wikipedia-style knowledge written ahead of time -- double check anything load-bearing before publishing widely.

landmarks.name: Box Hill
landmarks.question: Box Hill is part of which long-distance walking trail along the North Downs?
landmarks.fact: Box Hill is a chalk hill owned by the National Trust. It hosted the cycling road race for the 2012 London Olympics.

landmarks.name: Denbies Wine Estate
landmarks.question: Denbies Wine Estate, on the edge of Dorking, is one of England's largest what?
landmarks.fact: Denbies is one of the largest vineyards in England, set on the chalky slopes below Box Hill.

landmarks.name: St Paul's Church
landmarks.question: St Paul's Church in Dorking is an example of which era of English church building?
landmarks.fact: St Paul's was built in the Victorian era to serve Dorking's growing population.

landmarks.name: High Street
landmarks.question: Dorking's High Street is well known among collectors for having a high concentration of which kind of shop?
landmarks.fact: Dorking's High Street has long been known for its antique shops, giving the town a reputation among antique collectors.

landmarks.name: Fat Face
landmarks.question: Fat Face, a shop on Dorking High Street, mainly sells what?
landmarks.fact: Fat Face is a clothing and lifestyle retailer with a branch on the High Street.

landmarks.name: Dorking Museum
landmarks.question: Dorking shares its name with a distinctive breed of farm animal. What kind of animal is it?
landmarks.fact: Dorking Museum tells the story of the town, including the famous five-toed Dorking chicken breed associated with the area.

landmarks.name: Dorking Halls
landmarks.question: Dorking Halls is best known locally as a venue for what kind of events?
landmarks.fact: Dorking Halls is a community venue hosting theatre, cinema screenings, and concerts.

landmarks.name: Dorking Station
landmarks.question: Dorking is unusually well served by rail for its size. Roughly how many railway stations does the town have?
landmarks.fact: Dorking has three stations: Dorking (mainline), Dorking Deepdene, and Dorking West.

landmarks.name: Dorking Deepdene Station
landmarks.question: Dorking Deepdene station takes its name from the historic Deepdene estate nearby. What was Deepdene originally?
landmarks.fact: Deepdene was a grand landscaped estate near Dorking with gardens dating back centuries; the station nearby takes its name from it.

landmarks.name: Tennis Club
landmarks.question: In tennis, what is it called when a player wins a game without their opponent scoring a single point?
landmarks.fact: Dorking Lawn Tennis Club is one of the town's long-running sports clubs.

landmarks.name: Sainsbury's
landmarks.question: Sainsbury's, with a branch in Dorking, is one of the 'big four' supermarket chains in which country?
landmarks.fact: Sainsbury's is one of Britain's oldest and largest supermarket chains, founded in London in 1869.
